items.article_title: The Duration Mechanism: What Two Tipping Point Studies Mean for the Decisions You're Making Now
items.text:
The world's largest naval base floods from lunar cycles, not storms.

Climate news, adaptation, and speculation — every issue, free.
🔗 Link in bio

#ArcticIce #Biodiversity #EarthScience #ClimateData #OceanWarming

video_script.title: The Duration Mechanism: What Two Tipping Point Studies Mean for the Decisions You're Making Now
video_script.hook: The world's largest naval base floods from lunar cycles, not storms.
body_slides: Fifty-year piers. Thirty-year mortgages. The math assumes tomorrow looks like today.
Two studies. Same conclusion. The water isn't leaving.
Duration mechanism. The new permanent state has a name.
video_script.voiceover_text: The largest naval base on earth floods ten times a year from full moons alone. Two new studies suggest this isn't temporary adaptation stress. It's the new permanent. Fifty-year piers. Thirty-year mortgages. The math assumes tomorrow looks like today. Two studies. Same conclusion. The water isn't leaving. Duration mechanism. The new permanent state has a name. Every blueprint in Norfolk assumes the past. That's The Docket. Follow for more stories like this.
video_script.cta:
Follow @docketclimate for more
Climate news · Adaptation · Speculation
Link in bio
video_script.voiceover_cta: That's The Docket. Follow for more stories like this.
image_prompts: Wide shot of concrete pier construction at naval base, workers in hard hats, massive cranes against overcast sky, choppy gray water, industrial scale, documentary lighting, vertical orientation
Close-up of scientific research papers scattered on desk, highlighter marks on data charts, coffee-stained margins, fluorescent office lighting, stark shadows, vertical crop
Aerial view of flooded naval facility, water reflecting stormy sky, partially submerged vehicles, emergency lighting cutting through dusk, industrial wasteland aesthetic, vertical frame

items.text:
At a 7% discount rate, the EPA values a health benefit 30 years from now at 17 cents per dollar.

Climate news, adaptation, and speculation — every issue, free.
🔗 Link in bio

#ClimateInnovation #ClimateTech #Innovation #CarbonCapture #SustainableTech

video_script.title: Regulatory Impact Analysis: Projected Health Outcomes for Subject A, 2026–2082
video_script.hook: At a 7% discount rate, the EPA values a health benefit 30 years from now at 17 cents per dollar.
body_slides: EPA formula. Seven percent discount rate.
Forty-six years out. Three cents on the dollar.
Bakersfield air ranks dead last. Rescue inhaler, weekly.
video_script.voiceover_text: At a 7% discount rate, a health benefit forty-six years from now is worth three cents today. One parent in Bakersfield applied that EPA formula to their four-year-old's asthma. EPA formula. Seven percent discount rate. Forty-six years out. Three cents on the dollar. Bakersfield air ranks dead last. Rescue inhaler, weekly. Your child's lungs, discounted to market rate. More at The Docket. Follow to keep seeing these.
video_script.cta:
Follow @docketclimate for more
Climate news · Adaptation · Speculation
Link in bio
video_script.voiceover_cta: More at The Docket. Follow to keep seeing these.
image_prompts: Close-up of weathered hands holding a government form with financial calculations, harsh fluorescent office lighting, beige desk surface with coffee stains
Small plastic rescue inhaler sitting on a windowsill, hazy smog visible outside, late afternoon sunlight filtering through pollution
Wide shot of Bakersfield's industrial skyline through a child's bedroom window, air quality monitor glowing red, toys scattered on carpet below
